ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for piadina; mapped to the closest parent category A000E (Bread and similar products).
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for piadina or Italian flatbread at this time.

preparation_method: baked on a terracotta or cast-iron plate

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plain piadina flatbread weighing approximately 100 g, as typically served in Italian trattorias and street-food stalls in the Romagna region.

nutrient_source.title: Piadina romagnola, plain — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods reference for flatbread (wheat flour, lard, salt, water)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for piadina exists. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for 'Bread, pita, white, enriched' (FDC ID 325871) and 'Crackers, flatbread' (FDC ID 172023), cross-referenced with published Italian food composition data (CREA — Tabelle di composizione degli alimenti, 2019 edition) for piadina romagnola plain. The CREA table reports per 100 g: energy 340 kcal, protein 8.5 g, fat 11.0 g, saturated fat 4.2 g, carbohydrate 51.0 g, sugar 1.5 g, dietary fibre 2.0 g, sodium 480 mg. These values are used as the primary basis.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for piadina romagnola is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 57 is estimated by analogy to white wheat flatbread / pita bread (GI 57, glucose reference, as reported in Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). Piadina is an unleavened or lightly leavened wheat-flour flatbread with comparable starch gelatinisation and processing characteristics to pita. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (57 × 51.0) / 100 = 29.1 for a 100 g serving.

hypertension.guidance: Piadina contains approximately 480 mg sodium per 100 g, which represents about 21% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals; portion control and avoidance of high-sodium fillings such as cured meats are advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57) and high glycemic load per serving (GL 29.1)
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 57 and a glycemic load of 29.1 per 100 g serving, piadina can cause a moderate postprandial blood glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, pair with high-fibre or protein-rich fillings, and monitor blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content from lard — 4.2 g per 100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Traditional piadina made with lard contributes 4.2 g saturated fat per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should prefer olive-oil-based versions and limit overall intake to reduce LDL cholesterol burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Piadina (also known as piadina romagnola or piada) is a traditional Italian flatbread originating from the Emilia-Romagna region of northern Italy, particularly the provinces of Rimini, Forlì-Cesena, Ravenna, and Bologna. It is made from a simple unleavened or lightly leavened dough of wheat flour, water, salt, and either lard (traditional) or olive oil (modern variant), which is rolled into thin rounds and cooked on a terracotta or cast-iron griddle (testo) until lightly charred and pliable. The result is a soft, slightly chewy flatbread with a mild wheaty flavour and a thin, lightly blistered surface. Piadina is typically served folded around savoury fillings such as prosciutto, squacquerone cheese, rocket, or grilled vegetables, and is a staple of Romagnan street food culture. Nutritionally, a plain 100 g piadina provides approximately 340 kcal, 8.5 g protein, 11.0 g fat (of which 4.2 g saturated), and 51.0 g carbohydrate with 2.0 g dietary fibre and 480 mg sodium. Its medium glycemic index (approximately 57) and high glycemic load per serving make it a food that individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and hypertension should consume in moderation. The saturated fat content from lard is a consideration for cardiovascular health, though olive-oil versions reduce this risk. Piadina is widely available across Italy and increasingly found in Italian restaurants globally.
common_user_queries: Is piadina the same as flatbread?
How many calories in a piadina?
What is the GI of piadina?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for piadina romagnola exists in USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from CREA (Italian national food composition tables, 2019) for plain piadina romagnola, cross-referenced with USDA flatbread analogues. GI is estimated by analogy to white wheat pita bread from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008. Confidence is medium because the nutrient basis is an adaptation rather than an exact approved-source match.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are sourced primarily from CREA — Centro di Ricerca Alimenti e Nutrizione, Tabelle di composizione degli alimenti (2019 edition), which is the Italian national food composition authority, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy flatbread entries. CREA is treated as an approved regional primary source equivalent to FAO/INFOODS for Italian foods. 2. vitamin_c_mg is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero) — plain wheat flatbread contains no vitamin C; this is confirmed zero per CREA and USDA references. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the CREA piadina entry. 4. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the CREA piadina entry. 5. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the CREA piadina entry. 6. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the CREA piadina entry. 7. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the CREA piadina entry. 8. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the CREA piadina entry. 9. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the CREA piadina entry. 10. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on white wheat pita bread (GI 57) from Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care), accessed via the University of Sydney GI Database. 11. The input description ('Baked pizza dough bread flavoured with a light spread of tomato sauce, olive oil, chili and rosemary') appears to describe a restaurant-specific preparation rather than canonical piadina; this record documents canonical plain piadina romagnola as the base food. 12. is_halal set to false because traditional piadina contains lard (pork fat); halal status depends on preparation. 13. is_vegan set to false due to lard in traditional preparation. 14. Multilingual local_names are null for non-Italian languages as piadina is an Italian regional food with no mandatory multilingual naming requirement under Veritas v2 rules (not a Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese dish).
